evals.prompt: Write a test for my UserCard component that shows the user's name and email.
evals.expected_output: Use RTL with AAA pattern: render(<UserCard user={{ name: 'Alice', email: 'alice@test.com' }} />). Assert: expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Alice' })).toBeInTheDocument(). Prefer getByRole (accessibility-driven) over getByTestId. Test behavior, not implementation — don't check internal state.

evals.prompt: My tests are calling real APIs and sometimes fail due to network issues.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No real network usage in tests. Use MSW (Mock Service Worker) to intercept API calls. Create server.ts with handlers: rest.get('/api/users', (req, res, ctx) => res(ctx.json([...]))). Add server.listen() in setup, server.resetHandlers() in afterEach, server.close() in afterAll. Tests never call real APIs.

evals.prompt: I want to test that my form submits correctly when the user fills in all fields.
evals.expected_output: Use userEvent (async) for realistic browser simulation: const user = userEvent.setup(); await user.type(screen.getByLabelText(/email/i), 'test@test.com'); await user.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /submit/i })). Use findByText (async) for elements that appear after submission. Avoid fireEvent — it doesn't simulate browser behavior accurately.

should_not_trigger: Set up a Go gRPC interceptor
